adversarial.notes: Independently re-derived, not trusted from generator evidence: (1) `git diff --name-only HEAD` confirms exactly 2 tracked files changed (NIZAM.json, ecosystem/README.md), matching scope.files_modify exactly -- no undisclosed tracked change exists. (2) `.agent/evidence/032/diff.patch` byte-compared (`diff`) against a freshly captured `git diff HEAD` run in this QA pass: identical, confirming the generator's diff.patch is genuine and current, not stale. (3) Mutation probe on a disposable scratch copy of the real, post-implementation ecosystem/README.md: flipped the genuinely-correct '01_clean_state_preflight.md | ... | Shipped' row to 'Planned' (that file now genuinely exists on disk, landed for real by the concurrently-executing feature 033) and re-ran the contract's verification[9] dynamic Shipped/Planned check logic against the mutated copy: correctly FAILS, confirming the real implementation's 'Shipped' annotation is not an accident of a stale or hardcoded list but is genuinely, dynamically correct against the live filesystem, and that the check would have caught a wrong implementation. (4) Mutation probe on a disposable scratch copy of the real NIZAM.json with the newly-added 'ecosystem' module entry removed: `grep -q 'ecosystem'` STILL matches (pre-existing unrelated prose in registry/README.md's purpose text, standard/cross_repo_governance.md's summary, methodology/08's summary) -- independently re-confirming that the official feature_list_005.json acceptance-test command remains vacuous even post-implementation and was correctly never treated as the real gate by contract 032; the actually-enforced, non-vacuous check is verification[13] (NIZAM.json modules array contains exactly one path=='ecosystem' entry with key_documents EXACTLY ['ecosystem/README.md']), independently re-confirmed against the real file: exactly one such entry exists, with key_documents == ['ecosystem/README.md'] verbatim (no extra or dangling entries, notably NOT including the now-genuinely-existing ecosystem/01_clean_state_preflight.md landed by concurrent feature 033). No file in the working tree was altered by any of these probes (all operated on scratch copies under the session scratchpad, or a disposable untracked file immediately removed and confirmed via `git status --porcelain --untracked-files=all` to leave no residue).

observations: Independently re-ran all 19 contract (revision 1, approved) verification commands from repo root, extracted programmatically from .agent/contracts/032.json (not retyped, not trusted from .agent/evidence/032-verify-NN.txt in advance): all 19 exit 0 / PASS. final_verdict.summary: PASS. All 19 contract (revision 1) verification commands independently re-run from repo root and all exited 0/PASS, matching the generator's captured evidence (19 flat 032-verify-NN.txt files, each format-compliant with invocation-first/EXIT-last, and the consolidated .agent/evidence/032/verification.txt). The six content-gated entries that correctly failed pre-implementation in both contract-review rounds ([1]/[2] lifecycle, [5] version bump, [7] module navigation, [9] Shipped/Planned annotation, [13] structural NIZAM.json module entry) now correctly pass, confirming genuine implementation rather than a vacuous re-check. The evidence bundle is confirmed genuine and current: .agent/evidence/032/diff.patch byte-matches a freshly captured `git diff HEAD`, and that diff shows exactly the 2 files scope.files_modify authorizes (NIZAM.json, ecosystem/README.md). bash tools/validate.sh reports the full default sweep at SUMMARY: 11 passed, 0 failed (exit 0) -- no regression. Feature 032's acceptance criteria in .agent/feature_list_005.json are all satisfied with evidence traceable by path; the literal 'grep -q ecosystem NIZAM.json && validate.sh --target' AT text is confirmed, via a fresh mutation probe, to remain a vacuous match even post-implementation (pre-existing unrelated prose), so this verdict is issued on the actually-enforced, non-vacuous gate (verification[13], independently re-confirmed against the live NIZAM.json: exactly one well-formed 'ecosystem' module entry, key_documents EXACTLY ['ecosystem/README.md']) -- mirroring the .agent/qa/031.json precedent, the feature_list_005.json AT-text reconciliation ('[AMENDED ...]' in-place annotation) is flagged as a non-blocking orchestrator follow-up, not a defect in this feature's implementation. Adversarial mutation probes (Shipped/Planned flip on a scratch README copy; ecosystem-module removal on a scratch NIZAM.json copy) confirm the two most recently-hardened checks are genuinely live against the real, current implementation and filesystem state -- including a real, non-simulated confirmation that ecosystem/01_clean_state_preflight.md (landed for real by concurrently-executing feature 033 during this session) is correctly marked 'Shipped' in the README while correctly excluded from NIZAM.json's key_documents, exactly as scoped. No issues, no missing acceptance coverage, no unsupported claims found. Per AGF Sec 5.4 and this evaluator's own governing protocol, feature_list_005.json's F-032 status field, .agent/run_state.json, and every feature-033 surface were deliberately left untouched by this verdict -- feature-status flips are an orchestrator action performed after reading this qa_pass verdict.
